basics.name: Onyekachi Emenike
basics.label: Data Engineer · Data Scientist · ML Practitioner | Ph.D.
basics.summary: Data Engineer, Data Scientist, and ML Practitioner with a Ph.D. in Computational Mathematics. Builds production-grade ELT pipelines, layered cloud data warehouses (BigQuery, DuckDB, GCP), predictive ML models, and agentic AI applications using Python, SQL, dbt, Docker, and Terraform. Experienced across the full data stack — from batch ingestion and data modelling to MLOps and LLM-powered systems. Based in Germany, EU work authorisation, available immediately.

work.name: Berlin School of Business and Innovation
work.position: Lecturer — Data Analytics & Machine Learning MSc
work.summary: Designed and delivered graduate-level curriculum in applied statistics, machine learning, and data pipeline engineering for international postgraduate students. Mentored students on reproducible workflow design, model interpretability, and communicating quantitative results to non-technical stakeholders.
highlights: Developed project-based learning frameworks requiring students to build end-to-end analytical pipelines using Python, SQL, and cloud tools
Translated complex quantitative concepts into clear, decision-oriented frameworks for diverse non-technical audiences

work.name: Illinois Institute of Technology
work.position: Senior Research Associate / Visiting Assistant Professor
work.summary: Developed modular, reusable Python components for quasi-Monte Carlo simulation workflows, improving portability and code quality. Investigated algorithmic complexity scaling with error tolerance and dimensionality (Information-Based Complexity). Taught undergraduate Calculus to classes of 40 students.
highlights: Designed plug-and-play modules to improve quasi-Monte Carlo software efficiency and portability
Delivered lectures and designed assessments for undergraduate Calculus (40 students)

work.name: Johann Radon Institute for Computational and Applied Mathematics (RICAM)
work.summary: Built high-performance QMC algorithms in Python and MATLAB as part of an international research collaboration (Belgium, Austria, USA), resulting in 4 peer-reviewed journal publications. Served as teaching assistant for Mathematics for AI I, II & III at Johannes Kepler University (100 students per course).
highlights: 4 peer-reviewed journal publications in computational mathematics
Teaching assistant for Mathematics for AI I, II & III — 100 students per course

volunteer.summary: Designed a comprehensive curriculum for data science and AI tracks for the Girls in Tech programme, ensuring alignment with industry best practices.
highlights: Developed curriculum for data science and AI tracks
Created hands-on learning materials, case studies, and coding exercises
Empowered future tech leaders from underrepresented backgrounds
